book_metadata.title: Betrayal
book_metadata.subtitle: Power, deceit, and the fight for the future of the Royal family
book_metadata.author_line: From the number one bestselling author Tom Bower

cover.image_alt: A white background, with an image of Meghan Markle and Prince Harry cut diagonally. A roundel features with an image of the Royal Family and text that reads ‘author of the explosive Royal biography Revenge’. A quote from The Sunday Times in the bottom right hand corner reads ‘A master of intricacies’. The top line reads ‘From the number one bestselling author Tom Bower’, followed by the title, Betrayal in gold. Beneath, in black, the subtitle reads ‘Power, deceit, and the fight for the future of the Royal family’.

paragraphs.text: The fate of Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, still holds public attention. Much of that interest does not come from simple sympathy or dislike. People want to know whether the couple will keep succeeding or only manage to endure. Supporters and critics argue about the same question: how will this story end? Predictions of divorce, debt, and collapse follow them constantly. Some even say Harry is no longer the Royal Family’s “spare” but Meghan’s. In a Harper’s Bazaar interview for Christmas 2025, Meghan seemed to enjoy Harry’s devotion.

Since the couple moved to California in 2020, many have predicted that their marriage would fail. Some thought Harry would tire of the sunshine and miss Britain, his family, and his old friends. Others believed Meghan would grow bored with him and seek a richer, more powerful partner in Silicon Valley. So far, those predictions seem wrong. Yet almost every week, the Sussexes do something that starts the speculation again. Their story is driven by their constant search for publicity, especially through Meghan’s carefully managed public image.

While Meghan presents herself with increasing control, Harry has struggled to defend his royal status. He has little new to say, but he still refuses to admit the damage caused by Spare and his television interviews attacking his family. He is praised when he appears for charity, but criticized when he uses his title and birth for large fees. Meghan, meanwhile, has become more skilled in promoting herself as Her Royal Highness, the Duchess of Sussex. Her effort to build commercial success in California depends heavily on her own version of events, a version that critics say does not match the facts. Even repeated embarrassment and criticism have not weakened her ambition.

Five years earlier, my book Revenge described the Sussexes’ conflict with the Windsors and examined Meghan’s past. It argued that she cut off family, friends, and others as she pursued fame, and that she reshaped old events to build a new personal history. That process reached its peak in the 2021 Oprah Winfrey interview. Since then, Meghan has often seemed to distance herself from Harry except when appearing together helps publicity. At the same time, Harry’s own difficulties have grown. Far from Britain, with weaker charitable work and a more uncertain role, he appears increasingly isolated.

Now both the Sussexes and the monarchy face a turning point. The Royal Family has been weakened by scandal and illness, while Harry and Meghan remain trapped in the consequences of the choices they made after “Megxit.” The events of the last five years, along with new details about their lives before the break, make it necessary to take another hard look at Meghan, Harry, and the House of Windsor. In many ways, 2026 will be a decisive year for all of them. This book begins in the extraordinary week of Queen Elizabeth II’s death in September 2022.
